title: angelica dahurica var. formosana
intro: Angelica dahurica var. formosana, also known as Taiwanese angelica or Dong quai, is a variety of the Angelica dahurica plant native to Taiwan and other parts of Asia. It is a perennial herb used in traditional Chinese medicine, known for its medicinal properties in treating menstrual cramps, digestive issues, and other health problems. The plant's roots and rhizomes contain compounds such as ligustilide and butylphthalide, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antispasmodic properties, contributing to its health benefits, including reduced menstrual pain, improved digestion, and enhanced cardiovascular health. Angelica dahurica var. formosana can be prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
